broker_fee_detail: No-fee under NYC FARE Act; listed by landlord's agent
broker_message: Hi Mikhail — I'm very interested in 1011 Ocean Ave #4D. Before applying, could you confirm the floor and whether it's a walk-up, the square footage/floor plan, whether it's rent-stabilized with the legal rent, the lease term and total move-in costs, and whether utilities are included? I'm ready to move quickly if it's a fit. Thanks!
broker_questions: Is 4D a walk-up or is there an elevator? What floor exactly and does it face the street or rear?
What is the square footage and can I see a floor plan?
Is the unit rent-stabilized, and what is the legal registered rent vs. this asking rent?
What lease term is offered and what are the renewal terms?
What are total move-in costs (first month, security deposit, any fees)?
Has this unit or building had bedbug or pest treatment in the past year?
Are utilities (heat/hot water, cooking gas, electric) included or tenant-paid?
Is there in-unit or only building laundry, and where?
brokerage: Apartment Rentals and Sales Realty LLC
building_risk_rationale: Only 3 open HPD violations over 10 years — violations-per-unit better than the NYC average (0.81). No evictions, no recent Class C. One flag: an aggregator reports 7 bedbug filings in the past year, which contradicts its own 'no bedbug history' line and needs verification. Rent-stabilized / good-cause building, which adds tenant protection.
commute_notes: Q is an express to Midtown Manhattan in roughly 40 minutes; B (weekday) supplements. 2/5 within ~12 min adds an East Side option. Solid transit for outer Brooklyn.
concessions_to_ask: Confirm no move-in fees beyond security + first month
Longer lease at same rent to lock the below-market price
cons: Sparse listing (no sqft, no floor plan, only 6 images) hides layout/light quality
Likely top-floor walk-up in a 1931 building
Unverified 7 bedbug filings in the past year per an aggregator
leverage_notes: Rent is already below neighborhood median ($2,350) and at/under most comps, with no broker fee. Little room or reason to push on price; the play is to move quickly and lock a longer term rather than negotiate down.
listed_by: Mikhail Kayumov, Licensed Real Estate Salesperson
noise_risks: Ocean Avenue is a wide, moderately busy arterial — some traffic noise likely
plain_english: This is a well-priced no-fee studio in a low-risk, rent-stabilized Ditmas Park building with good B/Q access — a genuinely attractive combination that at $1,799 sits below the neighborhood median and most comps. It's only been listed 4 days, so it is NOT sitting; below-market no-fee studios move fast, so act quickly if it shows well. The listing is thin, though: verify the exact floor/walk-up status, square footage, rent-stabilized legal rent, and the bedbug question on the tour before applying. Don't expect to negotiate the price down — the value is already in your favor; instead lock it in and consider a longer lease.
pros: Below-market rent ($1,799 vs $2,350 neighborhood median) with no broker fee
Rent-stabilized / good-cause building: renewal rights and capped increases
Low building risk — violations per unit better than NYC average, no evictions; strong B/Q transit

red_flags.how_to_verify: Tour in person; ask for sqft and a floor plan
red_flags.issue: No square footage, floor plan, or interior detail beyond 5 photos
red_flags.why: Studio value hinges on layout and light; sparse listing hides whether it's genuinely 'large' as advertised

red_flags.how_to_verify: Confirm elevator vs walk-up with the agent
red_flags.issue: Likely 4th-floor walk-up (1931 building, 4 stories, no elevator listed)
red_flags.why: Top-floor walk-up affects daily livability and move-in

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask landlord for the building's annual bedbug (BBLNYC) filing and NYC's bedbug report
red_flags.issue: Aggregator reports 7 bedbug filings in past year (contradicts its own 'no history' line)
red_flags.why: Bedbug history is a real habitability concern if accurate

red_flags.how_to_verify: Request the DHCR rent registration history before signing
red_flags.issue: Rent-stabilized: advertised rent may exceed the legal registered rent
red_flags.why: A preferential rent could later reset, or the legal rent history could reveal overcharge

unverified: Exact live HPD open violation count and whether the '3' includes any Class B/C
The 7 bedbug filings figure (aggregator, internally inconsistent)
Whether unit is walk-up vs elevator and its true square footage
Legal rent-stabilized registered rent vs. advertised $1,799
